perturbation.original_text: 1. Confidential Information. "Confidential Information" shall mean and include any information which relates to the financial and/or business operations of each party, including, but not limited to, information relating to each party's customers, products, processes, financial condition, employees, manufacturing techniques, experimental work and/or trade secrets. Said Confidential Information is deemed proprietary by the parties hereto.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. Confidential Information. "Confidential Information" shall mean and include any information which relates to the financial and/or business operations of each party, specifically excluding any information relating to each party's customers, products, processes, financial condition, employees, manufacturing techniques, experimental work and/or trade secrets. Said Confidential Information is deemed proprietary by the parties hereto.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the common understanding and earlier inclusion criteria of 'Confidential Information' by explicitly excluding key elements previously covered. This creates an in-text contradiction, rendering the term's scope ambiguous and unenforceable as now the confidential information doesn't include the information relating to customers, products, processes, financial condition, employees, manufacturing techniques, experimental work and/or trade secrets
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Non-Disclosure of Proprietary Information. The parties agree that from the date of receipt, the party receiving the Confidential Information (hereinafter the "Receiving Party") shall not disclose Confidential Information to any other person, firm, corporation or other entity or use it for its own benefit except as provided in this Agreement. The Receiving Party shall not publish, divulge, communicate, or reveal any Confidential Information to any person, corporation, or other third party or to any of Receiving Party's employees who do not have a need to know such Confidential Information with respect to their job duties. The Receiving Party shall use the same degree of care to avoid publication or dissemination of the Confidential Information as it would with respect to its own confidential information. These efforts shall specifically include document control measures, such as numbered copies and sign out logs, and imposing on all employees, agents and other representatives of Receiving Party restrictions at least as strict as required by this Agreement. "Confidential Information" shall not include information, technical data or know-how which: (a) is already known to the Receiving Party at the time of disclosure and is not otherwise subject to restriction; (b) is or becomes publicly known through no wrongful act of the Receiving Party; (c) rightfully disclosed to Receiving Party by a third party who has no obligation of confidentiality to the Disclosing Party; (d) is independently developed by the Receiving Party; or (e) is approved for release by written authorization of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 1 explicitly excludes key elements (customers, products, processes, etc.) from the definition of 'Confidential Information' that were originally included. Section 3, regarding Non-Disclosure of Proprietary Information, relies on the original, broader definition of 'Confidential Information.' Therefore, the modified text directly contradicts Section 3 by narrowing the scope of what is considered confidential and subject to non-disclosure. The change renders the term's scope ambiguous and unenforceable. This is a strong in-text contradiction, consistent with the 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: 5. Return of Confidential Information. All Confidential Information and copies and extracts thereof shall be promptly returned to Disclosing Party three years from the effective date of this Agreement, or at any time within thirty (30) days of receipt of a written request by the Disclosing Party for the return of such Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Return of Confidential Information. All Confidential Information and copies and extracts thereof shall be promptly returned to Disclosing Party three years from the effective date of this Agreement, unless the Receiving Party deems it necessary to retain such information for ongoing projects, in which case no return is required.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces ambiguity by allowing the Receiving Party to unilaterally decide whether to return Confidential Information, directly contradicting the original mandatory return obligation. This in-text contradiction creates uncertainty about data retention policies and their enforcement, as now its no longer mandatory to return the information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. Term. This Agreement shall expire two (2) years from the Effective Date, but may be terminated prior to expiration by either party giving thirty (30) days' prior written notice to the other party; provided, however, the obligations to protect the Confidential Information in accordance with this Agreement shall survive for a period of five (5) years from the date of the last disclosure of Confidential Information is made under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 5 introduces an exception to the mandatory return of confidential information, stating that the Receiving Party can retain the information if deemed necessary for ongoing projects, negating the return obligation. This directly contradicts Section 9, which mandates the protection of Confidential Information for five years from the last disclosure date, implying an eventual and consistent protection and/or return policy. The Receiving Party's unilateral decision conflicts with this implied consistent treatment, leading to uncertainty and a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 6. Ownership of Information. The parties agree that any Confidential Information revealed to the other by the Disclosing Party remains the exclusive property of the Disclosing Party and its successors and assigns, unless otherwise expressly provided in writing signed by an authorized representative of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. Ownership of Information. The parties agree that any Confidential Information revealed to the other by the Disclosing Party may become the shared property of both parties if the Receiving Party incorporates the information into a new product or service.
perturbation.explanation: This alteration directly conflicts with the original statement that Confidential Information remains the 'exclusive property' of the Disclosing Party, creating an in-text contradiction. Now, the ownership is not exclusive, and can be shared. This conflict impacts ownership rights, making it unclear who owns what, especially in cases of product development. The contradiction occurs with the original assertion of exclusive ownership.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. Ownership of Information. The parties agree that any Confidential Information revealed to the other by the Disclosing Party may become the shared property of both parties if the Receiving Party incorporates the information into a new product or service.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies Section 6 to state that confidential information 'may become the shared property of both parties' under certain conditions. This directly contradicts the original text, which states that confidential information 'remains the exclusive property of the Disclosing Party.' The contradicted text is the changed text itself, highlighting that the alteration introduces a clear in-text contradiction regarding ownership rights. The perturbation explanation confirms that the alteration directly conflicts with the original text, making it unclear who owns the information.

perturbation.original_text: 8. Arbitration and Equitable Relief. (a) Arbitration. Except as provided in Section 8(b) below, the parties agree that any dispute or controversy arising out of or relating to any interpretation, construction, performance or breach of this Agreement shall be settled by arbitration to be held in San Diego County, California in accordance with the Commercial Arbitration Rules then in effect of the American Arbitration Association. The arbitrator may grant injunctions or other relief in such dispute or controversy. In the event of arbitration, a reasonable amount of discovery may be undertaken by the parties. The decision of the arbitrator shall be final, conclusive and binding on the parties to the arbitration. Judgment may be entered on the arbitrator's decision in any court having jurisdiction.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. Arbitration and Equitable Relief. (a) Arbitration. Except as provided in Section 8(b) below, the parties agree that any dispute or controversy arising out of or relating to any interpretation, construction, performance or breach of this Agreement shall be settled by mediation to be held outside of San Diego County, California. The mediator cannot grant injunctions or other relief in such dispute or controversy. In the event of mediation, no discovery may be undertaken by the parties. The decision of the mediator shall be non-binding on the parties to the mediation. Judgment cannot be entered on the mediator's decision in any court having jurisdiction.
perturbation.explanation: The modification replaces 'arbitration' with 'mediation' and significantly alters the dispute resolution process by changing the location, powers, and decision-binding nature of the process. This directly contradicts the original intent to have disputes resolved through binding arbitration in San Diego County. This creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. Arbitration and Equitable Relief. (a) Arbitration. Except as provided in Section 8(b) below, the parties agree that any dispute or controversy arising out of or relating to any interpretation, construction, performance or breach of this Agreement shall be settled by mediation to be held outside of San Diego County, California. The mediator cannot grant injunctions or other relief in such dispute or controversy. In the event of mediation, no discovery may be undertaken by the parties. The decision of the mediator shall be non-binding on the parties to the mediation. Judgment cannot be entered on the mediator's decision in any court having jurisdiction. (b) Equitable Remedies. The parties agree that it would be impossible or inadequate to measure and calculate the Disclosing Party's damages from any breach of the covenants set forth herein. Accordingly, the parties agree that if in the event of a breach of any of the covenants contained in this Agreement, the affected party will have available, in addition to any other right or remedy available, the right to obtain an injunction from a court of competent jurisdiction restraining such breach or threatened breach and to specific performance of any such provision of this Agreement. The parties further agree that no bond or other security shall be required in obtaining such equitable relief and the parties hereby consent to the issuance of such injunction and to the ordering of specific performance. (c) Legal Expenses. If any action or proceeding is brought for the enforcement of this Agreement, or because of an alleged or actual dispute, breach, default, or misrepresentation in connection with any of the provisions of this Agreement, the successful or prevailing party shall be entitled to recover reasonable attorneys' fees and other costs incurred in such action or proceeding in addition to any other relief to which it may be entitled.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'arbitration' with 'mediation', alters the location from San Diego County, removes the arbitrator's power to grant injunctions, prohibits discovery, makes the decision non-binding, and prevents judgment from being entered in court. The contradicted text is a restatement of the changed text, but also includes sections (b) and (c). Section (b) discusses equitable remedies, including the right to obtain an injunction, which directly contradicts the statement in the changed text that the mediator cannot grant injunctions. Furthermore, Section (c) allows for the recovery of attorney's fees in enforcement actions, which is inconsistent with the non-binding nature of mediation introduced in the changed text. The modification creates a strong in-text contradiction regarding the dispute resolution process and available remedies.

perturbation.original_text: 9. Term. This Agreement shall expire two (2) years from the Effective Date, but may be terminated prior to expiration by either party giving thirty (30) days' prior written notice to the other party; provided, however, the obligations to protect the Confidential Information in accordance with this Agreement shall survive for a period of five (5) years from the date of the last disclosure of Confidential Information is made under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. Term. This Agreement shall expire two (2) years from the Effective Date, and cannot be terminated prior to the expiration date. The obligations to protect the Confidential Information in accordance with this Agreement shall only survive for a period of one (1) year from the effective date of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This edit creates contradicting statements about the terms and expiration. One sentence states it expires in two years but the second states it can not be terminated prior to the expiration date, creating an ambiguiuty. One sentence the obligations to protect survive for 5 years and the second for 1 year. There's a clear contradiction around how long the information needs to be confidential.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. Term. This Agreement shall expire two (2) years from the Effective Date, and cannot be terminated prior to the expiration date. The obligations to protect the Confidential Information in accordance with this Agreement shall only survive for a period of one (1) year from the effective date of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies Section 9, which is then repeated in the Contradicted Text. The original text allowed for termination with 30 days' notice, while the changed text states the agreement cannot be terminated prior to expiration, creating a contradiction. Additionally, the original text stated confidentiality obligations survive for five years, while the changed text shortens it to one year, creating a clear contradiction within the same section and leading to ambiguity about the duration of the confidentiality obligations. The Perturbation Explanation highlights this exact inconsistency.

perturbation.original_text: 10. No Formal Business Obligations. This Agreement shall not constitute, create, give effect to, or otherwise imply a joint venture, pooling arrangement, partnership, or formal business organization of any kind, nor shall it constitute, create, give effect to, or otherwise imply an obligation or commitment on the part of either party to submit a proposal to or perform a contract with the other party. Nothing herein shall be construed as providing for the sharing of profits or loss arising out of the efforts of either or both parties. Neither party will be liable to the other for any of the costs associated with the other's efforts in connection with this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 10. Formal Business Obligations. This Agreement shall constitute a joint venture, and create an obligation and commitment on the part of both parties to submit a proposal to each other, and perform a contract. There shall be a sharing of profits or loss arising out of the efforts of both parties. Each party will be liable to the other for the costs associated with the other's efforts in connection with this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The entire section is modified to explicitly contradict the original statement that 'This Agreement shall not constitute... a joint venture'. By stating 'This Agreement shall constitute a joint venture', an immediate contradiction is introduced which implies conflicting obligations. This affects any understanding of the nature and scope of the parties' business relationship, generating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10. Formal Business Obligations. This Agreement shall constitute a joint venture, and create an obligation and commitment on the part of both parties to submit a proposal to each other, and perform a contract. There shall be a sharing of profits or loss arising out of the efforts of both parties. Each party will be liable to the other for the costs associated with the other's efforts in connection with this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text completely reverses the meaning of Section 10. The original text explicitly states that the agreement *does not* create a joint venture or any obligation to contract with the other party. The changed text states the exact opposite: that the agreement *does* constitute a joint venture and *does* create an obligation to contract. Given the contradictory nature of the change and the fact that the changed and contradicted texts are the same, a strong in-text contradiction exists.
